description:
-- Returns the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u — case-insensitive) in `s`.
local function countVowels(s: string): number
reference_solution:
local function countVowels(s: string): number
	local count = 0
	for c in s:lower():gmatch("[aeiou]") do
		count += 1
	end
	return count
end

	end

description:
-- Returns the sum of all numbers in the array `t`.
local function sumArray(t: {number}): number
reference_solution:
local function sumArray(t: {number}): number
	local total = 0
	for _, v in t do
		total += v
	end
	return total
end

description:
-- Returns true if `s` is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards,
-- ignoring case and non-alphanumeric characters).
local function isPalindrome(s: string): boolean
reference_solution:
local function isPalindrome(s: string): boolean
	local cleaned = s:lower():gsub("[^a-z0-9]", "")
	return cleaned == cleaned:reverse()
end

description:
-- Returns the factorial of non-negative integer `n`.
-- Raises an error if n < 0.
local function factorial(n: number): number
reference_solution:
local function factorial(n: number): number
	assert(n >= 0, "n must be non-negative")
	if n == 0 then return 1 end
	local result = 1
	for i = 2, n do result *= i end
	return result
end

reference_solution:
local function filter<T>(t: {T}, predicate: (T) -> boolean): {T}
	local result: {T} = {}
	for _, v in t do
		if predicate(v) then
			table.insert(result, v)
		end
	end
	return result
end

local function deepEqual(a: any, b: any): boolean
	if type(a) ~= type(b) then return false end
	if type(a) ~= "table" then return a == b end
	for k, v in a do if not deepEqual(v, (b :: any)[k]) then return false end end
	for k in b do if (a :: any)[k] == nil then return false end end
	return true
end

local function check(label: string, got: any, expected: any)
	if deepEqual(got, expected) then
		print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@PASS:" .. label)
	else
		print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@FAIL:" .. label)
	end
end

check("evens", filter({1,2,3,4,5,6}, function(x) return x % 2 == 0 end), {2,4,6})
check("none", filter({1,3,5}, function(x) return x % 2 == 0 end), {})
check("all", filter({2,4,6}, function(x) return x % 2 == 0 end), {2,4,6})
check("empty", filter({}, function(x: number) return x > 0 end), {})

reference_solution:
local function unique(t: {number}): {number}
	local seen: {[number]: boolean} = {}
	local result: {number} = {}
	for _, v in t do
		if not seen[v] then
			seen[v] = true
			table.insert(result, v)
		end
	end
	return result
end

description:
-- Splits string `s` by delimiter `delim` (a plain string, not a pattern)
-- and returns an array of the resulting substrings.
-- Empty segments between consecutive delimiters ARE included.
local function split(s: string, delim: string): {string}
reference_solution:
local function split(s: string, delim: string): {string}
	local result: {string} = {}
	local pattern = "([^" .. delim:gsub("[%(%)%.%%%+%-%*%?%[%^%$]", "%%%1") .. "]*)" .. delim:gsub("[%(%)%.%%%+%-%*%?%[%^%$]", "%%%1")
	local last = 1
	while true do
		local i, j = s:find(delim, last, true)
		if not i then break end
		table.insert(result, s:sub(last, i - 1))
		last = j + 1
	end
	table.insert(result, s:sub(last))
	return result
end

	for k, v in a do if not deepEqual(v, (b :: any)[k]) then return false end end
	for k in b do if (a :: any)[k] == nil then return false end end
	return true
end

end

check("leading", split(",a,b", ","), {"","a","b"})
check("multi-char delim", split("a::b::c", "::"), {"a","b","c"})

description:
-- Returns the nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1).
-- Must handle n up to at least 30 efficiently (no naive recursion).
local function fib(n: number): number
reference_solution:
local function fib(n: number): number
	if n <= 0 then return 0 end
	if n == 1 then return 1 end
	local a, b = 0, 1
	for _ = 2, n do a, b = b, a + b end
	return b
end

local function flatten(t: {any}): {any}
	local result: {any} = {}
	for _, v in t do
		if type(v) == "table" then
			for _, inner in v :: {any} do
				table.insert(result, inner)
			end
		else
			table.insert(result, v)
		end
	end
	return result
end

	for k in b do if (a :: any)[k] == nil then return false end end

reference_solution:
local Stack = {}
Stack.__index = Stack

function Stack.new<T>(): Stack<T>
	return setmetatable({ _data = {} :: {any}, _size = 0 }, Stack) :: any
end

function Stack:push(value: any)
	self._size += 1
	self._data[self._size] = value
end

function Stack:pop(): any?
	if self._size == 0 then return nil end
	local v = self._data[self._size]
	self._data[self._size] = nil
	self._size -= 1
	return v
end

function Stack:peek(): any?
	return self._data[self._size]
end

function Stack:size(): number
	return self._size
end

function Stack:isEmpty(): boolean
	return self._size == 0
end

end

local s = Stack.new()
check("new isEmpty", s:isEmpty(), true)
check("new size", s:size(), 0)
s:push(10)
s:push(20)
check("size after push", s:size(), 2)
check("peek", s:peek(), 20)
check("pop first", s:pop(), 20)
check("pop second", s:pop(), 10)
check("empty after pops", s:isEmpty(), true)
check("pop empty", s:pop(), nil)

description:
-- Returns true if `n` is a prime number (n >= 2 to be prime).
local function isPrime(n: number): boolean
reference_solution:
local function isPrime(n: number): boolean
	if n < 2 then return false end
	if n == 2 then return true end
	if n % 2 == 0 then return false end
	for i = 3, math.sqrt(n), 2 do
		if n % i == 0 then return false end
	end
	return true
end

end

check("4", isPrime(4), false)
check("17", isPrime(17), true)
check("100", isPrime(100), false)
check("1", isPrime(1), false)
check("0", isPrime(0), false)
check("97", isPrime(97), true)

local function deepCopy<T>(value: T): T
	if type(value) ~= "table" then return value end
	local copy = {} :: any
	for k, v in value :: any do
		copy[deepCopy(k)] = deepCopy(v)
	end
	return copy
end

	for k in b do if a[k] == nil then return false end end

description:
-- Implement a generic Queue (FIFO) using Luau OOP.
-- Methods: enqueue(value), dequeue() -> value?, peek() -> value?,
--          size() -> number, isEmpty() -> boolean
-- The implementation must be O(1) amortised for both enqueue and dequeue.
type Queue<T> = {
	enqueue: (self: Queue<T>, value: T) -> (),
	dequeue: (self: Queue<T>) -> T?,
	peek: (self: Queue<T>) -> T?,
	size: (self: Queue<T>) -> number,
	isEmpty: (self: Queue<T>) -> boolean,
}
local Queue = {}
reference_solution:
local Queue = {}
Queue.__index = Queue

function Queue.new<T>(): Queue<T>
	return setmetatable({ _data = {} :: {any}, _head = 1, _tail = 0 }, Queue) :: any
end

function Queue:enqueue(value: any)
	self._tail += 1
	self._data[self._tail] = value
end

function Queue:dequeue(): any?
	if self._head > self._tail then return nil end
	local v = self._data[self._head]
	self._data[self._head] = nil
	self._head += 1
	return v
end

function Queue:peek(): any?
	return self._data[self._head]
end

function Queue:size(): number
	return self._tail - self._head + 1
end

function Queue:isEmpty(): boolean
	return self._head > self._tail
end
test_harness:
--!strict
{{CODE}}

local function check(label: string, got: any, expected: any)
	if got == expected then print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@PASS:" .. label)
	else print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@FAIL:" .. label .. ":got " .. tostring(got) .. " expected " .. tostring(expected)) end
end

local q = Queue.new()
check("new isEmpty", q:isEmpty(), true)
q:enqueue("a")
q:enqueue("b")
q:enqueue("c")
check("size 3", q:size(), 3)
check("peek a", q:peek(), "a")
check("dequeue a", q:dequeue(), "a")
check("dequeue b", q:dequeue(), "b")
check("size 1", q:size(), 1)
q:enqueue("d")
check("dequeue c", q:dequeue(), "c")
check("dequeue d", q:dequeue(), "d")
check("empty after", q:isEmpty(), true)
check("dequeue empty", q:dequeue(), nil)

description:
-- Given a table `t`, returns a new table with keys and values swapped.
-- If multiple keys map to the same value, the last key (in iteration order)
-- wins.
local function invertTable<K, V>(t: {[K]: V}): {[V]: K}
reference_solution:
local function invertTable<K, V>(t: {[K]: V}): {[V]: K}
	local result = {} :: {[any]: any}
	for k, v in t do
		result[v] = k
	end
	return result
end

	else print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@FAIL:" .. label .. ":got " .. tostring(got) .. " expected " .. tostring(expected)) end
end

local inv = invertTable({a=1, b=2, c=3})
check("a->1", inv[1], "a")
check("b->2", inv[2], "b")
check("c->3", inv[3], "c")
local empty = invertTable({})
check("empty length", next(empty), nil)

description:
-- Implement `memoize`: wraps a single-argument function and caches its
-- results. Subsequent calls with the same argument return the cached value
-- without calling the underlying function again.
local function memoize<T, U>(fn: (T) -> U): (T) -> U
reference_solution:
local function memoize<T, U>(fn: (T) -> U): (T) -> U
	local cache = {} :: {[any]: any}
	return function(arg: T): U
		if cache[arg] == nil then
			cache[arg] = fn(arg)
		end
		return cache[arg]
	end
end
test_harness:
--!strict
{{CODE}}

local callCount = 0
local function expensive(n: number): number
	callCount += 1
	return n * n
end

local memoExpensive = memoize(expensive)

local r1 = memoExpensive(5)
local r2 = memoExpensive(5)
local r3 = memoExpensive(10)

local function check(label: string, got: any, expected: any)
	if got == expected then print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@PASS:" .. label)
	else print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@FAIL:" .. label .. ":got " .. tostring(got) .. " expected " .. tostring(expected)) end
end

check("first call", r1, 25)
check("cached call", r2, 25)
check("different arg", r3, 100)
check("call count", callCount, 2)

description:
-- Implement `pipe`: takes an array of single-argument functions and returns
-- a new function that applies them left-to-right.
-- pipe({f, g, h})(x)  ≡  h(g(f(x)))
local function pipe<T>(fns: {(T) -> T}): (T) -> T
reference_solution:
local function pipe<T>(fns: {(T) -> T}): (T) -> T
	return function(x: T): T
		local v = x
		for _, fn in fns do
			v = fn(v)
		end
		return v
	end
end

end

local double = function(x: number): number return x * 2 end
local addOne = function(x: number): number return x + 1 end
local square = function(x: number): number return x * x end

local f = pipe({double, addOne, square})
check("pipe(3)", f(3), 49)
check("empty pipe", pipe({})(42), 42)
check("single fn", pipe({double})(5), 10)

description:
-- Simulate a simple Roblox-style signal/connection system (no actual Roblox APIs).
-- Implement: Signal.new() returning a signal with methods
--   :Connect(callback) -> connection (with :Disconnect())
--   :Fire(...) -- calls all connected callbacks with given args
--   :Once(callback) -> connection  -- auto-disconnects after first fire
local Signal = {}
test_harness:
--!strict
{{CODE}}

local function check(label: string, got: any, expected: any)
	if got == expected then print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@PASS:" .. label)
	else print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@FAIL:" .. label .. ":got " .. tostring(got) .. " expected " .. tostring(expected)) end
end

local sig = Signal.new()
local count = 0
local conn = sig:Connect(function() count += 1 end)

sig:Fire()
check("fired once", count, 1)
sig:Fire()
check("fired twice", count, 2)
conn:Disconnect()
sig:Fire()
check("disconnected", count, 2)

local onceCount = 0
sig:Once(function() onceCount += 1 end)
sig:Fire()
check("once fired", onceCount, 1)
sig:Fire()
check("once not again", onceCount, 1)

reference_solution:
local StateMachine = {}
StateMachine.__index = StateMachine

function StateMachine.new(states: {[string]: any}, initial: string)
	assert(states[initial], "Initial state not found")
	local sm = setmetatable({ _states = states, _current = initial }, StateMachine)
	local enterFn = states[initial].onEnter
	if enterFn then enterFn() end
	return sm
end

function StateMachine:current(): string
	return self._current
end

function StateMachine:send(event: string): boolean
	local state = self._states[self._current]
	if not state then return false end
	local nextState = state.transitions and state.transitions[event]
	if not nextState then return false end
	local exitFn = state.onExit
	if exitFn then exitFn() end
	self._current = nextState
	local enterFn = self._states[nextState] and self._states[nextState].onEnter
	if enterFn then enterFn() end
	return true
end
test_harness:
--!strict
{{CODE}}

local function check(label: string, got: any, expected: any)
	if got == expected then print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@PASS:" .. label)
	else print("@@LUAU_BENCH_RESULT@@FAIL:" .. label .. ":got " .. tostring(got) .. " expected " .. tostring(expected)) end
end

local log = {}
local sm = StateMachine.new({
	idle   = { onEnter = function() table.insert(log, "enter:idle")   end, transitions = { start = "running" } },
	running = { onEnter = function() table.insert(log, "enter:running") end,
	            onExit  = function() table.insert(log, "exit:running")  end, transitions = { stop = "idle", pause = "paused" } },
	paused  = { onEnter = function() table.insert(log, "enter:paused")  end, transitions = { resume = "running" } },
}, "idle")

check("initial", sm:current(), "idle")
check("bad event", sm:send("stop"), false)
check("still idle", sm:current(), "idle")
check("start", sm:send("start"), true)
check("running", sm:current(), "running")
check("pause", sm:send("pause"), true)
check("paused", sm:current(), "paused")
check("enter idle in log", log[1], "enter:idle")
check("enter running in log", log[2], "enter:running")
